events.title: Weather tracker: hail covers parts of Tunisia and Algeria like snow
events.summary:
Accumulations of up to 3cm deep reported as severe thunderstorms also bring heavy downpours to central Italy

Severe thunderstorms have affected the Mediterranean this week. On Monday, a surface low-pressure system in the Mediterranean in conjunction with an upper air cut-off low, led to thunderstorms over north Africa. Their intensity was aided by the hot precursor conditions.

Algeria and Tunisia were notably affected by the thunderstorms, with some hail accumulation layers as a result. When so much hail forms, it starts to lay down sheets of hail, covering the ground like snow. Hail accumulations of up to 3cm were reported in Oum Ladjoul and Hammam Sokhna in Algeria, and there were hailstones of up to 3cm in diameter in Makthar, Tunisia. Thunderstorms continued in the region through the following day, with further hail accumulations, notably in Ouled Bousmir, Tunisia, where there was a layer about 2cm deep.

events.title: Jacinta Allan wants voters to see Victoria’s 12-year-old Labor government as ‘new and united’. Can she cling to power?
events.summary:
With a reshuffled cabinet, the premier is hoping to quell leadership rumblings as her party seeks an unprecedented fourth term

  • Get our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcast

As the Victorian premier, Jacinta Allan, stood alongside the fresh faces in her reshuffled cabinet on Wednesday, she attempted to send her increasingly jaded electorate a blunt message: despite its 12 years in power, her government is – apparently – new.

In her opening four-minute preamble to reporters, Allan - whose Labor government will in November seek an unprecedented fourth term - repeated the word 17 times. In one sentence alone, she referred to her “new cabinet”, “new portfolios”, “new solutions” and “new areas that are going to drive this government forward”.

Benita Kolovos is Guardian Australia’s Victorian state correspondent

Its northern section is made up of cavernous brick warehouses called capannoni, which in the 16th century could produce a warship a day through a rigorously ordered assembly line.

“We have just concluded the last leg of our engagements following productive and fruitful visits … where we held meaningful bilateral discussions aimed at strengthening cooperation across key areas,” Dar said on X.

events.summary:
US president’s desperation for war to end has seen him trying to speed through a process he does not fully control

A set of mismanaged and premature media announcements by Donald Trump and Tehran has led to the collapse of progress towards a peace settlement between Iran and the US.

The recent missteps ended with Iran saying it would reinstate a complete block on the movement of commercial shipping through the strait of Hormuz and that it would not allow any of its stockpile of highly enriched uranium to be exported out of the country.

IRGC reportedly fires on tanker as it tries to pass through strait during brief window when shipping lane had reopened

• Middle East crisis – live updates

Iranian officials say they have reversed the reopening of the strait of Hormuz and reimposed restrictions on the vital shipping lane after the US said it would not end its blockade of Iranian ports.

A UK maritime agency reported that Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) ships had fired at a tanker as it attempted to pass through the strait on Saturday. Reuters reported an Indian-flagged vessel carrying crude oil had also been attacked while in the waterway.

events.title: US extends waiver allowing countries to buy Russian oil – as it happened
events.summary:
This blog is now closed – follow live coverage on our new blog

In case you’re just joining us, here are the latest developments in the Middle East to bring you up to speed. It’s 9am in Beirut and Jerusalem, 9.30am in Tehran and 2am in Washington DC.

A 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Lebanon has come into effect, pausing fighting between Israel and Hezbollah that has killed more than 2,100 Lebanese people and displaced more than 2.1 million. The agreement was announced earlier by Donald Trump, who said he had spoken with Israeli prime minister Benjamin Netanyahu and Lebanese president Joseph Aoun, and invited both leaders “for meaningful talks” at the White House. Both leaders welcomed the agreement.

Israel and Hezbollah both maintained their right to defend themselves if the truce is broken – here’s our full report.

Netanyahu called the ceasefire a “historic” opportunity for peace but refused to withdraw his troops from southern Lebanon during the pause in fighting. “We are remaining in Lebanon in an expanded security zone,” he said, due to the “danger of an invasion” and to prevent fire into Israel. “That is where we are, and we are not leaving.”

UN chief António Guterres welcomed the ceasefire, which took effect at midnight on Thursday (2100 GMT) in Lebanon, and urged “all actors” to fully respect it. He hoped the halt in fighting would “pave the way for negotiations”.

The Lebanese army warned people displaced from southern Lebanon about returning home because of intermittent shelling that was reported after the ceasefire came into effect.

The Israeli military warned residents of southern Lebanon not to return south of the Litani River despite the truce.

Iran’s foreign ministry spokesperson welcomed the ceasefire and stressed it was already part of the original Iran-US agreement brokered by Pakistan.

Israel and Hezbollah continued to exchange fire in the hours before the truce took effect.

Continue reading...

events.title: India fails to pass bill to boost women’s representation after delimitation row
events.summary:
Opposition accuses Narendra Modi government of using quotas as cover for redrawing electoral map

The Indian government has failed to pass a bill to increase female representation in parliament after being accused of using the plan as a guise to redraw the country’s electoral map.

It was the first time in 12 years in power that a constitutional amendment proposed by Narendra Modi’s Bharatiya Janata party (BJP) government was not passed by parliament.

Continue reading...

events.title: Indian music legend Asha Bhosle dies aged 92
events.summary:
Two-time Grammy nominee was one of Bollywood’s most versatile and celebrated voices

The Indian singer Asha Bhosle, whose voice defined Bollywood cinema and whose career spanned almost eight decades, has died in Mumbai at the age of 92.

Bhosle, who recorded more than 12,000 songs, became her country’s pre-eminent exponent of playback singing – recording tracks that were then lip-synced on film by actors. She also boldly embraced cabaret and western-influenced melodies to forge a distinctive musical identity.

Continue reading...

events.title: Islamabad prepares to host historic negotiations between Iran and the US
events.summary:
In Pakistan’s capital, the army has been deployed, a public holiday has been declared and the streets are eerily empty

  • Middle East crisis – live updates

The streets of Islamabad were on strict lockdown as Pakistan’s capital prepared to play host to historic negotiations between Iran and the US that have dangled the promise of an end to war that has devastated the Middle East.

Even as the US-Iran ceasefire looked increasingly precarious, amid Israel’s continued bombardment of Lebanon and disputes over the terms of the talks, Pakistani officials insisted that the make-or-break peace negotiations would be going ahead over the weekend as planned.

Continue reading...

events.title: Netanyahu says there is no ceasefire in Lebanon as Israel launches fresh strikes
events.summary:
Israeli PM says he will continue to attack Hezbollah ‘with full force’ after attacks that killed more than 300 people

  • Middle East crisis – live updates

Benjamin Netanyahu has said there is “no ceasefire in Lebanon” and Israel would continue “to strike Hezbollah with full force” as the country’s military launched fresh strikes.

The Israeli prime minister’s remarks and latest attacks on what the IDF called “Hezbollah launch sites” came shortly after Donald Trump said he had asked Netanyahu to be more “low-key” in Lebanon.

Later on Friday, a US state department official said Israel and Lebanon will hold talks in Washington next week. The announcement came as Netanyahu ordered his ministers to seek direct talks with Lebanon focused on disarming Iranian-backed Hezbollah.

Continue reading...

events.title: How Pakistan secured ‘biggest diplomatic win in years’ with Iran ceasefire
events.summary:
Analysts say Pakistani officials’ efforts led to breakthrough that has helped avert catastrophe, at least for now

  • Middle East crisis – live updates

Pakistan’s leaders had almost lost hope. After more than two weeks of frantic negotiations, phonecalls and diplomatic summits to try to end the US-Israeli war with Iran, it looked like the conflict might instead be escalating into Islamabad’s worst nightmare.

In a cabinet meeting held at about 5pm on Tuesday, Pakistan’s prime minister, Shehbaz Sharif, was morose. “We should brace ourselves for the impact of the war,” he told his cabinet ministers. “The situation has really become very bleak. The chance of peace has become dim.”

Continue reading...

events.title: Queen Elizabeth II’s official biographer named as historian Anna Keay
events.summary:
The author, who will interview members of royal family for book, says being chosen for role is a ‘profound honour’

Anna Keay, a historian whose most celebrated book is about Britain’s republican period, has been confirmed as Queen Elizabeth II’s official biographer.

Keay will interview members of the royal family and the late queen’s friends and servants. She will also have access to the monarch’s personal and official papers held in the royal archives.

events.title: Kensington Gardens reopens after police deem suspicious items non-hazardous
events.summary:
Officers responded after group claimed to have targeted nearby Israeli embassy with ‘dangerous substances’

Kensington Gardens in London has reopened after the discovery of several suspicious items including two jars containing a powdered substance that was deemed to be non-hazardous, police said.

Officers in protective clothing responded to an incident near the Israeli embassy on Friday after counter-terrorism police investigated a video shared online in which a group claimed to have targeted the embassy with drones carrying “dangerous substances”.

events.title: Starmer would have blocked Mandelson role over vetting failure, says Lammy
events.summary:
Deputy prime minister says it is ‘inexplicable’ top civil servant kept Downing Street in dark

Keir Starmer would have blocked Peter Mandelson from serving as the UK’s ambassador to Washington had he known he failed security vetting, David Lammy has said, as he attempted to shore up the prime minister amid damaging fallout from the row.

In his first public comments on the vetting affair, Lammy said it was “inexplicable” that Oliver Robbins, the former top civil servant who was forced out of the Foreign Office this week, had opted to leave Downing Street in the dark over the outcome.

events.title: Two drivers killed in motorway crash in Perth and Kinross
events.summary:
Two men died at the scene after head-on motorway collision near Kincross, Police Scotland say

Two drivers have died in a motorway crash in Scotland involving a car apparently travelling in the wrong direction on the carriageway, police have said.

The two men died at the scene of the collision on the M90 near Kinross, a town in Perth and Kinross, at 10.30pm on Friday.

events.title: Two more Reform local election candidates accused of offensive posts
events.summary:
Labour calls on Nigel Farage to sack candidates and says his party’s checks ‘clearly not fit for purpose’

Reform UK’s checks on candidates are “clearly not fit for purpose”, Labour has said after two more candidates in May’s local elections were accused of making offensive or potentially racist social media posts.

Meanwhile, it emerged that Restore Britain, the party set up by the MP Rupert Lowe after he left Reform, appeared to have accepted a donation from someone who has called publicly on social media for “another Hitler” to come to power.

events.title: United plane makes emergency landing in Pittsburgh over ‘possible security issue’
events.summary:
Passengers on flight from Chicago to New York exited via slides as FBI, bomb technicians and K-9 unit responded

  • Sign up for the Breaking News US email to get newsletter alerts in your inbox

A United Airlines flight bound for New York City was forced to land and evacuate in Pittsburgh on Saturday morning after crew members reported a “possible security issue”, a spokesperson for the Federal Aviation Administration said.

United flight 2092 took off from Chicago O’Hare airport and had been en route to LaGuardia airport in New York. The plane diverted to Pittsburgh international airport as a result of the reported threat.

Continue reading...

events.title: Two US army soldiers injured by brown bear during training exercise in Alaska
events.summary:
Soldiers were participating in ‘land navigation training’ when bear injured them in a ‘defensive attack’

Two US army soldiers have been injured after encountering a brown bear in a mountainous training area in Anchorage, Alaska, the military said on Friday.

The incident happened on Thursday as the soldiers were participating in a “land navigation training event” in Arctic valley, part of the joint base Elmendorf-Richardson’s training area.

events.title: Three people sentenced to jail for bear-suit insurance scam in California
events.summary:
State investigated claims of bear attacks on cars, unconvincingly backed up by video of person in bear suit

When it comes to the California department of insurance, don’t poke the bear.

That is the lesson three individuals in Los Angeles learned recently when they were sentenced to jail time for an insurance fraud scheme in which they staged attacks on high-end vehicles by having a person dress up in a bear costume – then pretending that person was an actual bear.

events.title: Father of man who inspired Super Mario was also named Luigi, researcher finds
events.summary:
Elisabeth Zetland, a senior researcher at MyHeritage, found that the actual Luigi had immigrated to US from Italy

Gaming enthusiasts have known for years that Nintendo named its mustachioed, superhero plumber after the company’s landlord, Washington state businessman Mario Arnold Segale.

But it has only just been determined that Nintendo may have unknowingly named Super Mario’s fictional brother after Segale’s real-life father: Luigi, whose biography evokes that of millions of 20th-century US immigrants from Italy.

Continue reading...

events.title: Trump announces reforms to accelerate access to psychedelic drug treatments
events.summary:
President signed executive order directing FDA to expedite review of psychedelic drugs including ibogaine

Donald Trump on Saturday announced reforms intended to speed up access to medical research and treatment based on psychedelic drugs.

The president signed an executive order directing the federal Food and Drug Administration (FDA) to expedite review of drugs such as ibogaine, which US military veteran groups have said can help treat post-traumatic stress disorder.

events.summary: The Indian prime minister blamed the main opposition party for failing to pass a law reserving a third of parliamentary seats for women. The plan was tied to a redrawing of voting boundaries.

events.summary: The US has renewed a waiver permitting countries to buy Russian oil for another month. The decision came two days after the Trump administration had said it had no plans to extend the waiver.

events.title: Can the 25th Amendment remove Donald Trump from office?

events.summary: A 13-year-old stumbled upon a coin from ancient Troy — an extremely rare find in Germany. Experts say it may offer new clues about little-known links between ancient Greece and northern Europe.

events.summary: The Polish government had urged organizers "not to make public space available to promoters of a criminal ideology." Kanye has disavowed and apologized his previous pro-Nazi stance, linking it to his bipolar disorder.

events.title: India’s harvest festivals under climate strain

events.title: Cyprus: Global politics felt at Candidates chess tournaments
events.summary: The FIDE Candidates tournaments are two of the most important events in the chess calendar. This year, they took place in Cyprus. But tension relating to Russia and the war in the Middle East was never too far away.

events.title: Chess: Uzbekistan's new star shows Asia's continued rise
events.summary: Javokhir Sindarov of Uzbekistan has earned the right to challenge India's Dommaraju Gukesh for the world title. Both men are under 21 and from Asia, underlining an ongoing demographic shift in top level chess.

events.title: Nueva Germania: The failed 'Aryan project' in Paraguay

events.title: Will Bulgaria's election change the country's course?
events.summary: Bulgarians will vote in their eighth parliamentary election in five years on April 19. Former President Rumen Radev is projected to win. Radev, who has a record of pro-Russian stances, is pledging to fight corruption.

events.title: Several killed in Russian strikes across Ukraine, many more injured
events.summary: A 12-year-old was among those killed as Russian missile and drone attacks hit Ukrainian cities of Kyiv, Odesa and Dnipro.

events.title: Senegal: The Goethe-Institut opens its new building in Dakar
events.summary: The Goethe-Institut's new building in Dakar champions sustainable architecture, made from the earth. Star architect Francis Kere has blended local materials with contemporary design.

events.title: Anatomy of the clitoris: 3D images reveal its nerve system
events.summary: New 3D images reveal the clitoris’s complex nerve network, highlighting long‑standing gaps in medical understanding of the female body.

events.title: Top EU court rules online gamblers can sue for compensation if betting illegal in home country
events.summary: The ECJ has ruled that online gamblers can seek compensation if they lost money when gambling was illegal in their home country, even if the laws have since changed. This could pave the way for others to reclaim losses.

events.title: Germany-Israel divide deepens after Merz criticism
events.summary: The Israeli finance minister's sharp criticism of the German chancellor's views on Israel's settlement policy is just the latest sign of a growing estrangement. What's the current state of German-Israeli relations?

events.title: US jury finds Live Nation, Ticketmaster hold illegal monopoly
events.summary: The verdict will not bring immediate relief to concertgoers frustrated by high ticket prices, but could cost the company hundreds of millions of dollars.

events.title: World Cup 2026: Fans with disabilities 'excluded'
events.summary: Complaints that the 2026 World Cup, hosted largely by the USA, is a rip off have been widespread and constant. But it's not just the prices that are making it feel impossible for football fans with disabilities.

events.title: Germany news: Teacher group urges action over pupil violence
events.summary: A German teachers' group says decisive action is needed to counter a surge in violence in classrooms and corridors. Meanwhile, the Greens say a blanket speed limit on major highways would help save fuel.

events.title: UK signs deal to rejoin EU's Erasmus exchange scheme
events.summary: Britain will rejoin the popular Europe-wide student exchange program six years after leaving it.

events.summary: The comprehensive reform package aims to plug a multibillion-euro hole in Germany's expensive health care system. Reforms include mandatory second opinions for costly surgeries and no more coverage for homeopathy.

events.title: Attacks on US academics: A microcosm of a larger threat to democracy

events.title: Solar power in Morocco's desert: Bold vision, mixed results
events.summary: A massive solar tower in the Moroccan desert is the beacon of an ambitious push for a clean energy future. But fossil fuels and grid constraints stand in the way.

events.title: Women's workwear: When proper fit becomes a safety issue
events.summary: With limited options, many women in trades and industry have to wear men's workwear. But a poor fit causes discomfort and can compromise safety. Now, some firms are stepping up to design safer, better-fitting gear.

events.title: From Kipling to OnlyFans: how sex work has been named, framed and debated across history and language.
events.summary: A Bonn exhibition traces the cultural history of sex work and shows how the words used to describe it have shaped stigma, policy and lived experience.

events.title: Hungary: What's next for Peter Magyar after election win?
events.summary: Peter Magyar has promised nothing less than a "system change" in Hungary. The expectations both at home and abroad are huge. DW explores what Magyar is likely to do on the most pressing issues facing Hungary.

events.title: France's Renault slashes engineer jobs amid pressure from Chinese carmakers
events.summary: The French carmaker said it would cut up to 2,400 engineering jobs as the company tries to catch up to Chinese electric vehicles in terms of price and production speed.

events.title: Spain: Sanchez government finalizes amnesty plan for undocumented migrants
events.summary: Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez's government hailed the plan as "an act of justice and a necessity." It will allow unauthorized migrants to apply for temporary residence permits instead, and to start paying taxes.

events.title: Lufthansa pilots to strike again for 2 more days, leading to a full working week of disruption
events.summary: Pilots at Germany's Lufthansa airline will strike again on Thursday and Friday, the Vereinigung Cockpit trade union said. Cabin crew are also striking this week, clouding the company's centenary celebrations

events.title: Sudan: Iran war prompts 'massive' food, fuel price rises; drones kill hundreds of civilians in 2026

events.title: Hungary: Orban election loss reverberates across Central Europe
events.summary: Viktor Orban's crushing defeat in the April 12 election will have far-reaching consequences for Europe's political landscape — and especially for neighboring Czechia and Slovakia, which are run by his political allies.

events.title: The world ditched wasteful toilets, the US stayed behind
events.summary: US toilets use far more water than many of their global counterparts. President Donald Trump is pushing to loosen water pressure standards, a move critics say would increase waste.

events.title: Spanish PM Sanchez' wife charged with corruption
events.summary: The case is based on a complaint by an anti-corruption group with far-right ties, and is the latest in a string of corruption allegations which have plagued Sanchez' inner circle.

events.title: EU applauds Hungary's election result
events.summary: After Hungary’s opposition ousted Viktor Orban after 16 years in power, hopes rise in Brussels that Budapest may abandon its obstructionist course. What is the EU expecting from Peter Magyar?

events.summary: Pilots who are members of the Vereinigung Cockpit trade union are striking on Monday and Tuesday. As hundreds of flights were canceled, the UFO cabin crew union said it would also strike on Wednesday and Thursday.

events.summary: A US naval blockade cuts off Iran's main source of hard currency by halting most of its oil exports. Will President Trump's pressure force Tehran back to negotiations or widen the conflict?

events.summary: Viktor Orban's Fidesz party was swept aside in Hungary's parliamentary election. His defeat is a blow for Putin, Trump and populists the world over. Election winner Peter Magyar is promising major changes.

events.title: How much sway does Trump have over Hungarian voters?
events.summary: US President Trump has backed Hungarian PM Orban. It's unclear whether the endorsement will help in Hungary's election — the European hard-right has struggled with Trump's recent anti-Europe policies and threats.

events.title: UFOs: 'Most are reported by people out for an evening smoke'
events.summary: Hansjürgen Köhler has been a volunteer UFO hunter for more than 50 years. He sees his work as scientific investigation, but sometimes also as caring for others.

events.title: Russian Memorial human rights NGO labeled 'extremist'
events.summary: Russia's Supreme Court has labeled the human rights organization Memorial as "extremist." Experts say this represents a new level of repression in Russia.

events.title: Ukrainian civilians abducted, tortured in Russian prisons
events.summary: Russia has been holding thousands of Ukrainian civilians behind bars for years, in breach of international humanitarian law. Families have been fighting for their loved ones to be released, some for more than a decade.

events.title: Is this Syrian doctor one of the 80% Germany wants to remove?
events.summary: Chancellor Friedrich Merz has ignited a debate by declaring that 80% of Syrians would be repatriated. Does this mean that Germany also intends to deport those who are integrated and whom the country needs?

events.title: First gay rights movement: Berlin's wild 1920s queer history
events.summary: Before the Nazis took power, Berlin was a center of LGBTQ+ rights and queer culture, with research institutes, a flourishing nightlife and one of the earliest gay rights' movements.

events.title: German Bundeswehr says military-aged men can travel freely
events.summary: A new rule requiring men to get permission to travel abroad recently put Defense Minister Boris Pistorius under pressure. He says he has fixed the situation, but the criticism continues.

events.title: NASA's billion-dollar space race goes into overdrive
events.summary: NASA's renewed push to get a human mission to the moon and Mars is neither cheap, nor for the faint of heart. But who ends up covering the costs and what does everyone still on Earth get out of all this?

events.title: Germany: Police suspect antisemitism in Israeli restaurant attack in Munich
events.summary: Police and restaurant owners believe antisemitism motivated the Munich attack. Nobody was hurt, but three windows were broken, causing damage worth several thousand euros. This comes amid tension over the war in Iran.

events.title: Recycling's next big thing — or big bluff?
events.summary: An advanced recycling process claims it can handle hard-to-recycle plastics, like packaging. But critics say it's not worth the effort.

events.title: Lufthansa cabin crew begin strike
events.summary: Hundreds of flights have been canceled as cabin crew members walk out. This is Lufthansa's third major strike this year.

events.title: Has Coachella festival sold out?
events.summary: One of the world's biggest open-air music and art festivals has been hit by controversies and declining credibility — yet the world's biggest acts continue to headline.

events.title: Germany: Fare evasion is a crime that can send you to prison
events.summary: In Germany, riding without a ticket on public transportation can land you in jail. The justice minister would like to change this, but her conservative coalition partners are skeptical.

events.title: Veteran German actor Mario Adorf dies aged 95
events.summary: Few actors had a career as rich as Mario Adorf. He played legendary Hollywood villains, wrote German TV history and still had film roles at age 90.

events.title: Artemis II crew emotional as mission prepares for reentry
events.summary: In less than 48 hours, the Orion spacecraft is due to splash down in the Pacific Ocean. Crew members have said what they saw in space will forever change how they see the world.

events.title: Hungary election: How football has helped Orban keep power
events.summary: Viktor Orban is a football fanatic but has long used the sport as a political tool, both in Hungary and abroad. With polls predicting election defeat, he could also lose his chance of hosting the Champions League final.

events.title: Norway's Telenor faces lawsuit for giving junta data from its customers in Myanmar
events.summary: A Swedish non-profit has filed a class action lawsuit against Norway's Telenor, accusing it of endangering customers in Myanmar by sharing their data with the junta. The company said it had no real choice.

events.title: How farmers are facing a fertilizer shortfall
events.summary: The war in Iran has sparked a global fertilizer shortage and is threatening food security. What are farmers around the world doing to save their crops?

events.title: Should Western influencers promote Taliban‑run Afghanistan?
events.summary: In the race for clicks, likes and followers, some travel content creators are being criticized for promoting Afghanistan while ignoring the harsh reality of Taliban rule.

events.title: 60 years after the Indonesian mass killings: Is the Cold War back?
events.summary: Six decades after helping the Indonesian army crush one of the biggest communist parties in the world, the US is once again meddling in countries it considers a thorn in its flesh. Is history repeating itself?

events.title: Will AI doom office space to history?
events.summary: Artificial intelligence's potential for disruption is being felt across multiple industries. Now commercial real estate is feeling the heat from what analysts say is a "paradigm shift."

events.title: Homegrown energy is slashing US energy bills
events.summary: In times of skyrocketing energy prices, some US citizens are making big savings by producing electricity at home.

events.title: One in five young Germans plan to leave the country
events.summary: A recent study shows that as many as one in five Germans between the ages of 14 and 29 are actively thinking about moving to another country — and 40% plan to do so in the long term.

events.title: Can small nuclear reactors solve Europe's energy woes?
events.summary: Supporters say modular reactors are key to meeting energy goals, but critics say they're a costly distraction.

events.title: Mediterranean sharks are vanishing in a legal void
events.summary: Along Libya's coast, sharks — including breeding females — are being sold unchecked. With little enforcement and growing financial pressure on fishermen, vulnerable species are spiraling toward extinction.

events.title: 'Drill baby drill' ― Trump opens up nature to big energy
events.summary: America's beloved national parks and public lands face conservation rollbacks and sell-offs as the administration of US President Donald Trump pushes for fossil fuel and timber extraction.

events.title: Semenya hits out at impact of Olympic ban on DSD athletes
events.summary: Caster Semenya has had her gender scrutinized in public for years after winning two Olympic golds. She has hit out at what she sees as a policy that disproportionately affects athletes with sexual differences.

events.title: Wealth tax: Why are countries afraid to tax the ultrarich?
events.summary: Growing income inequality and public displays of billionaire power are increasing calls for new income and wealth taxes aimed at the world's richest individuals. Can wealth ever be taxed fairly?

events.title: How US 'Liberation Day' tariffs reshaped global trade
events.summary: One year after Trump's "Liberation Day" tariffs, global trade flows have shifted. Trade data shows which countries gained, which lost — and who is footing the bill.

events.title: UNESCO World Heritage sites facing the heat
events.summary: From Easter Island to the Great Wall of China, many of the world's most iconic cultural heritage sites are under threat from rising temperatures linked to climate change.

events.title: World Cup 2026: Iraq qualify but Iran doubts loom
events.summary: Iraq have overcome tough circumstances to reach the World Cup for the first time in 30 years, with the lineup now complete. But Iran's potential withdrawal still hangs over a tournament fraught with political issues.

events.title: FIFA World Cup 2026: Trump's politics put off German fans
events.summary: Many German fans are torn on whether to travel to the US for this summer's major football tournament.

events.title: Tiger Woods 'stepping away' for treatment after DUI arrest
events.summary: Tiger Woods has announced a break from golf four days after he crashed his SUV and was arrested for driving under influence. Fans had been awaiting his return at the Masters this month.

events.title: Were Grandma and Grandpa Nazis?
events.summary: What did our forebears do during the Nazi era? A subject long shrouded in silence in German families. Now, the US National Archives has made millions of NSDAP membership cards available online.

events.title: FIFA World Cup: Which German players will make the final cut?
events.summary: Several players on Germany's national football team have really stood out during the squad's recent test matches ahead of this summer's major soccer tournament.

events.title: After the Iran war, how fast could global trade recover?
events.summary: The Iran war could end soon as a ceasefire goes into effect. But the true test will be how quickly energy, fertilizer and other supply chains can bounce back whenever the Strait of Hormuz is reopened.

events.title: Germany beat Ghana to complete confidence-boosting March
events.summary: A late winner from Stuttgart's Deniz Undav secured a second friendly win in a week for Germany. With the FIFA World Cup just 10 weeks away, Julian Nagelsmann's team will be starting to believe they can win it all.

events.title: Air Canada CEO to retire after English-only condolence furor
events.summary: Air Canada has announced that CEO Michael Rousseau will retire later this year. This follows public criticism of his failure to voice condolences in French as well as English after two pilots were killed in a collision.

events.title: The pleasure of books in the digital age
events.summary: The digitization of global literature has brought vast libraries to our fingertips. But the quiet power of a physical book offers a rare escape from constant digital noise.

events.title: Amnesty flags FIFA World Cup human rights risks
events.summary: The human rights group has sounded the alarm about risks faced by visitors to the 2026 FIFA World Cup. It goes as far as to describe the situation in the United States as a "human rights emergency."

events.title: Solar is winning the energy race
events.summary: The world's cheapest power source is scaling at warp speed, pushing coal, gas and nuclear aside.

events.title: At 40, Lady Gaga isn't done revolutionizing pop music
events.summary: As iconic as she is outlandish, Lady Gaga has left quite a mark on the world of music. Here's how she reinvented pop — and reached millions by opening up about her trauma and illness.

events.title: Florian Wirtz inspires Germany past Switzerland
events.summary: Germany beat Switzerland 4-3 in an entertaining World Cup warm-up in Basel. Two goals and two assists from Liverpool's Florian Wirtz secured a confidence-boosting victory for Julian Nagelsmann's team.

events.title: Daylight saving time: A history of fact, folklore and fuss
events.summary: From wartime strategy to Halloween lobbying lore, the legacy of daylight saving time is tangled in culture, commerce and contested claims.

events.title: Fighting in Iran: Is another 1970s‑style oil crisis looming?
events.summary: Supply disruptions, high prices, nervous markets: The current war in the Middle East brings back memories of the oil crises of the 1970s. Back then, many economies plunged into stagflation. How bad is the current crisis?

events.title: 'Looksmaxxing' — the manosphere beauty cult
events.summary: The online trend with roots in misogyny encourages socially isolated young men to undertake painful practices to gain beauty and status — and its language is reaching the White House.

events.title: Smart sleepers could be the glow-up night trains need
events.summary: Europe has a bit of an on-off relationship with night trains. A Berlin team's smart cabin concept could lead to greater commitment.

events.title: World Cup 2026: Cost, Trump and travel give fans pause
events.summary: As teams battle it out for the final six spots, loyal fans are making plans for World Cup 2026. DW spoke to supporters across the world to hear about five-figure expenditure, visa uncertainty and safety concerns.

events.title: Iran war boosts strategic significance of South Caucasus
events.summary: With war raging in Iran, the South Caucasus has become one of the few stable corridors between Asia and Europe. Nowhere are the conflict's opportunities and risks more visible than in Azerbaijan.

events.title: Germany's green reputation hits a crossroads
events.summary: Germany's new climate spend is big on wind power and e‑mobility. Some call it real momentum, others say it’s basically a green band‑aid.

events.title: Is VW eyeing Iron Dome parts production at German plant?
events.summary: A report in the Financial Times says Volkswagen is considering producing defense components for Israel. A VW spokesperson told DW the company was exploring "viable options."

events.title: Is Iran cashing in millions from Strait of Hormuz blockade?
events.summary: An Iranian lawmaker claims Tehran is quietly charging tankers up to $2 million for safe passage through the Strait of Hormuz. The move threatens to turn the key waterway into a high-stakes toll booth.

events.title: From Goethe to Soraya: German-Iranian stories
events.summary: It's hard to imagine now, but Iran and Germany were once surprisingly close. A master poet, a German "Lawrence of Arabia," and an empress all played a role in this shared history.

events.title: WNBA's labor deal is a structural shift for women's sports
events.summary: America's top women's basketball league just agreed to a new CBA. It will have a significant impact on women's sport.

events.title: NASA plans moon base instead of orbital lunar station
events.summary: New NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman says the US space agency will pivot from launching an orbiting lunar station to constructing a base on the moon's surface. It comes amid a renewed moon race with Russia and China.

events.title: Who wins what in the EU's new trade deal with Australia?
events.summary: Squeezed by Trump's tariffs and China's industrial muscle, the EU and Australia bury old trade disputes to finalize a long-delayed free trade deal.

events.title: American apocalypse: The end 'feels personal and imminent'
events.summary: Social psychologists in the US found that a third of people surveyed feel the world will end in their lifetimes. That's not only depressing; it also affects people's sense of agency when faced by war or climate change.

events.title: Australia and EU strike free trade deal
events.summary: The European Union and Australia have agreed on a sweeping new trade deal, compromising to boost exports in the face of global uncertainty over trade.

events.title: Argentina's 'Dirty War' still on trial 50 years later
events.summary: Some 30,000 people were "disappeared" under the brutal military dictatorship that ruled Argentina from 1976 to 1983. Victims are still fighting for justice.

events.title: Report: Hungary using more Russian oil, despite EU phase out
events.summary: A new report by the Center for the Study of Democracy, shared exclusively with DW, says that Hungary has increased its dependence on Russian oil since 2021. It has also become highly dependent on Russian gas.

events.title: Asian markets tumble, but Europe, US rally on Trump optimism
events.summary: Markets in Asia nosedived as trading restarted on Monday. Europe's indices were headed in the same direction, until optimistic Iran comments from Donald Trump prompted traders in Europe and the US to start buying again.

events.title: Iran's energy sites still on Trump's target list
events.summary: US President Trump has delayed strikes on Iran's power plants for five days, citing "productive" talks with Tehran. DW asks what infrastructure US forces could still bomb to force a reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: How Africa gets shortchanged in trade with Europe
events.summary: The establishment of free trade between much of both continents for half a century has created growth in many areas. However, the data analysis shows that Africa remains on unequal footing.

events.title: Iranians and Israelis united through music
events.summary: The Berlin-based music ensemble Sistanagila brings together musicians from Iran and Israel who offer a message of hope for a better future.

events.title: Alexandra Popp and Borussia Dortmund: A sign of the times
events.summary: Germany's most famous female footballer has signed for a third-division team. The transfer will have huge impacts across the women's game in Germany.

events.title: Earth's climate more unbalanced than ever, WMO warns
events.summary: The world's oceans have broken heat records for nine straight years, glaciers are retreating and extreme weather is killing thousands. The only way to avoid the worst is to urgently ditch fossil fuels.

events.title: Navigation satellites guide the world — and its wars
events.summary: From smartphone maps to precision weapons, satellite navigation underpins modern life and war. Global navigation satellite systems have become indispensable, and that's made them vulnerable to disruption and attack.

events.title: Using every drop: Physics answers a crucial kitchen question
events.summary: How long does it take for the last drop of milk or oil to drip out of the bottle? US scientists have probed this everyday conundrum.

events.title: An answer to US drought conditions may be in the toilet
events.summary: Americans are willing to fork out extra money each month to drink recycled wastewater. With climate change intensifying drought in several regions, cities are exploring ways to turn sewage into drinking water.

events.title: French UNIFIL soldier killed in Lebanon, Hezbollah responsible, says Macron
events.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron announced the death of a French UN peacekeeping soldier Saturday following an attack that also left three people wounded in southern Lebanon, saying that "everything suggests" Hezbollah being responsible for the attack. The Iran-backed group denied involvement.

events.title: Europe's far right leaders gather in Milan rally against immigration
events.summary: Several thousand people gathered in Milan on Saturday for a large rally attended by several European far-right leaders who spoke on immigration, security, and against EU regulations.

events.title: Pope Leo XIV condemns 'logic of extractivism' in Angola visit
events.summary: Pope Leo XIV denounced the “social and environmental disasters” linked to a “logic of extractivism” on Saturday, the first day of his visit to Angola, a country marked by decades of exploitation of its vast resources.

events.title: Colombia fights female genital mutilation
events.summary: In Colombia’s Risaralda region, female genital mutilation persists in some Indigenous Embera communities, despite severe health risks including infection, complications in childbirth and death. For the first time, Congress is debating a bill to ban the practice, led in part by Indigenous women calling for its eradication.

events.title: Replay : Donald Trump holds briefing amid Hormuz tension
events.summary: Rewatch Donald Trump’s live briefing from the White House on Saturday, April 18, as Iran announced it was re-closing the Strait of Hormuz while the US maintains its port blockade. FRANCE 24's Andrew Hilliar has the analysis.

events.title: At least five dead in Kyiv shooting, suspected gunman killed
events.summary: At least five people were killed in a shooting Saturday in a residential neighborhood of Kyiv, and 10 were hospitalized, Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky announced. The suspected gunman was shot during an arrest attempt, officials said.

events.title: UNIFIL sheds light on southern Lebanon attack that killed French soldier
events.summary: A French soldier was killed in southern Lebanon on Saturday 18, while serving with UNIFIL, the United Nations peacekeeping force. UNIFIL deputy spokesperson Tilak Pokharel was FRANCE 24’s guest this afternoon to explain what happened and what is currently known about those responsible.

events.title: No signs of regime collapse as Iran weathers weeks of war
events.summary: After weeks of US and Israeli strikes, Iranians are watching to see if ceasefire talks could lead to negotiations, while uncertainty over the future of the conflict persists. Despite foreign calls for regime change, analysts say Iran’s leadership remains entrenched, as economic pressure, repression and war damage deepen tensions inside the country.

events.title: Hormuz tensions highlight pressure tactics in US-Iran negotiations
events.summary: H. A. Hellyer, senior fellow in geopolitics at the Royal United Services Institute, was France 24’s guest and analysed the renewed tensions between the United States and Iran in the Strait of Hormuz as a strategy of mutual pressure amid ongoing negotiations. While talks are set to continue in Islamabad, both sides are using leverage, particularly the US blockade of Iranian port, to strengthen their positions, making any quick breakthrough unlikely.

events.title: Turkey’s economy hit as Syrians return home
events.summary: Since the end of the war in Syria last year, many Syrians have left Turkey to return home. Of the 3.6 million refugees living in the country, more than half a million are believed to have already gone back. These departures are weakening the Turkish economy, which relies heavily on low-cost labour, particularly in the textile and agricultural sectors. In the south-east, in Gaziantep, half of the garment workshops closed last year due to a shortage of workers. A report by Jenna Le Bras and Adrià Rocha Cutiller.

events.title: French soldier killed in attack on UN mission in southern Lebanon, officials say
events.summary: A French soldier was killed and three others wounded ​while clearing a road in southern Lebanon in an attack that UNIFIL peacekeepers and French officials said on Saturday was likely carried out ​by ‌Iran-backed Hezbollah. In calls with Lebanese President ⁠Joseph Aoun and Prime Minister Nawaf Salam, French President Emmanuel Macron condemned the "unacceptable attack", his ‌office said in a statement. Renée Davis, FRANCE 24's correspondent, has the latest from Beyrouth.

events.title: Strait of Hormuz : Iran to block transit until US lifts its blockade
events.summary: Tensions continue to rise in the Middle East, with Iran briefly reopening the Strait of Hormuz before closing it again after around 20 hours. Tehran says the US is not respecting the terms of the agreement reached, casting further doubt on the fragile deal. Meanwhile, negotiations are still ongoing in Islamabad. FRANCE 24’s correspondent in Tehran, Reza Sayah, brings the latest developments.

events.title: Middle East : Fragile US-Iran talks hinge on nuclear and Hormuz disputes
events.summary: US-Iran negotiations show limited progress but remain tense, with disputes over enriched uranium and the Strait of Hormuz still unresolved. Donald Trump warns the conflict could resume if no deal is reached, while mediators say compromises may still be possible on nuclear restrictions.

events.title: Sudan’s war leaves thousands of children alone and displaced
events.summary: Sudan is entering its fourth year of civil war between the army and the Rapid Support Forces, a conflict the UN calls the world’s largest humanitarian crisis, with children among the main victims. In Darfur and beyond, thousands are displaced, many separated from their families, facing hunger, violence and deep trauma despite overwhelmed aid efforts.

events.title: Should Timmy, the whale stranded off the German coast, be saved?
events.summary: Rescuers in northern Germany are trying to save a sick humpback whale nicknamed 'Timmy', which has repeatedly become stranded off the Baltic Sea coast. Experts are using air cushions and tugboats to refloat the animal, amid debate over whether it can still be safely rescued.

events.title: Lens keep Ligue 1 title hopes alive with comeback win over Toulouse
events.summary: Lens came back from 2-0 down to claim a crucial home win over Toulouse on Friday night. The title-chasers are now just one point adrift of PSG, who have two games in hand.

events.title: Pope vs Trump: Has the week of tension weakened the US president?
events.summary: A week of escalating tensions has unfolded between Donald Trump and the Vatican, after Trump insulted the Pope on social media following remarks on peace in the Middle East. His behaviour shocked audiences worldwide and reignited debate over his mental health. The feud has further exposed divisions within the American right in a country where religion plays a central political role. FRANCE 24’s guest Scott Lucas, Professor of US and international politics at the University College of Dublin, examined whether this week of tensions has ultimately weakened the US president.

events.title: Sudan war enters fourth year as the humanitarian crisis deepens
events.summary: Sudan has entered its fourth year of devastating conflict, after fighting broke out in April 2023 between the Sudanese army and the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces. The war has left millions displaced and, according to the UN, created the world’s largest humanitarian crisis.

events.title: In Liberia, concerns are growing over gold mining pollution
events.summary: In Liberia, communities are raising alarm over rising water and soil pollution linked to gold mining activities. In the village of Jikandor, north of the capital, residents accuse the Bea Mountain Mining Corporation of violating environmental standards and contaminating rivers through undisclosed spills.

events.title: The Gaza Strip grapples with a major garbage, health crisis
events.summary: In Deir al-Balah, displaced families are living next to growing rubbish piles as destroyed infrastructure forces authorities to rely on unsafe temporary dumps. Across the Gaza Strip, poor sanitation and pest infestations are raising serious health concerns despite limited humanitarian aid.

events.title: Middle East : What to expect from the truce between Lebanon and Israel ?
events.summary: The United States and Israel announced on Thursday evening that a ceasefire agreement had been reached in Washington between Lebanon and Israel, following historic direct talks between the two countries. Dany Moudallal, a journalist at the Lebanese daily L’Orient-Le Jour, spoke to France 24 to analyse the stakes surrounding this fragile truce announcement.

events.title: Ethiopia journalist abduction sparks press freedom fears
events.summary: The managing editor of one of Ethiopia’s last independent media outlets remains missing after being kidnapped by masked men inside the Addis Standard newsroom on Wednesday. Ethiopia, already one of the biggest jailers of journalists in sub-Saharan Africa after Eritrea, is seeing a renewed crackdown ahead of June’s elections. International media have had their accreditation revoked, and for local journalists, the stakes are even higher.

events.title: Angola prepares for the arrival of Pope Leo XIV
events.summary: As part of his African tour, Pope Leo XIV is visiting Angola, where around 40% of the population is Catholic. The visit is highly significant for the country’s large Catholic community, who see it as an important moment of recognition and connection with the Vatican.

events.title: Iran closes Hormuz Strait again over US blockade with ships mid-transit
events.summary: Iran's military declared the Strait of Hormuz closed again on Saturday, its military command said, hours after reopening it and with more than a dozen commercial ships passing through the vital waterway. The toing and froing over the strait cast doubt on US President Donald Trump's optimism the day before, that a peace deal to end the US-Israeli war with Iran was "very close". FRANCE 24's Reza Sayah has the latest from Tehran.

events.title: At least six killed in Kyiv shooting
events.summary: At least six people have been killed after a gunman opened fire in Kyiv.

events.title: 'Very interesting documents' related to UFOs will be released 'soon', says Trump
events.summary: Donald Trump has promised some "very interesting documents" related to UFOs will be released by the US defence department "very, very soon".

events.title: Canadian 'online poison seller' to have 'murder charges dropped in plea deal'
events.summary: A Canadian chef accused of supplying deadly substances to people around the world who have taken their own lives will have murder charges dropped as part of a plea deal, his lawyer has said.

events.title: Fourth Kanye West concert cancelled
events.summary: Another Kanye West concert has been cancelled amid a furore over his past antisemitic comments.

events.title: Cuba is on its knees - and 'next' on Trump's list
events.summary: At the start of this year, Donald Trump ordered the capture and removal of Venezuela's president Nicolas Maduro - he's now facing a trial in New York.

events.title: Why are England fans facing paying $150 for a 15 minute train ride?
events.summary: England fans are facing having to pay train fares of more than £100 to watch one of their World Cup group matches in the USA.

events.title: Daniel Kinahan arrested over alleged links to 'international organised crime'
events.summary: Irish national Daniel Kinahan has been arrested in Dubai over alleged links to an "international organised crime network".

events.title: Medal-winning veteran facing Afghan war crime charges released on bail
events.summary: Australia's most decorated living veteran has been released on bail after being charged with war crimes linked to the Afghanistan war more than ‌a decade ago.

events.title: Eight dead after helicopter crash
events.summary: A helicopter has crashed in Indonesia, killing all eight people on board.

events.title: Missing wolf that became internet sensation is found
events.summary: A wolf that escaped form a South Korean zoo, becoming an internet sensation in the process, has been found.

events.title: South Koreans breath sighs of relief as escaped wolf is returned to zoo safely
events.summary: The South Korean internet as erupted in celebration as a 2-year-old wolf that escaped from a zoo in South Korea was safely captured Friday after a nine-day search that drew national attention

events.title: UK leader Starmer says he is 'furious' he wasn't told Peter Mandelson had failed security checks to be ambassador to US
events.summary: UK leader Starmer says he is 'furious' he wasn't told Peter Mandelson had failed security checks to be ambassador to US

events.title: Even the dead must make way as construction transforms Afghanistan's capital
events.summary: Across Afghanistan's capital, buildings are being razed to make way for major construction to modernize Kabul's heavily congested streets

events.title: Families in Iran struggle with school closures even as the airstrikes have stopped
events.summary: Schools have been closed across Iran since the United States and Israel launched the war on Feb. 28, with no word on when in-person instruction will resume

events.title: World Court celebrates 80-year anniversary as the international legal framework is under strain
events.summary: The United Nations secretary-general warns that international law is eroding

events.title: Senior British official resigns over Mandelson appointment but Starmer insists he won't go
events.summary: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer has no plans to resign over the appointment of Peter Mandelson as U.K. ambassador to Washington, despite Mandelson failing security checks

events.title: A fragile calm in Lebanon as a US-brokered truce holds and families head home
events.summary: A fragile calm has settled over parts of Lebanon as a 10-day ceasefire brokered by the United States takes hold between Israel and Hezbollah

events.title: Airline adding bunk beds for economy travelers but bans snacks, smells and cuddling
events.summary: Air New Zealand will soon add triple-tier bunk beds for economy travelers to have a chance to sleep on long-haul flights

events.title: Macron says a French soldier was killed and 3 were wounded in attack on peacekeepers in Lebanon
events.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron says a French soldier was killed and three others wounded in an attack on U.N. peacekeepers in southern Lebanon

events.title: UK police charge 3 in arson attack at Persian-language media organization
events.summary: British police have charged three people in an arson attack at a Persian-language media organization in northwest London

events.title: Patients and dental community mourn Dr. Cerina Fairfax, killed by Virginia's ex-lieutenant governor
events.summary: Patients and colleagues in the Virginia dental community are remembering Dr. Cerina Fairfax as a devoted mother, a caring dentist and the rock at the center of her family, after she was killed by her estranged husband, former Virginia Lt. Gov. Justin F...

events.title: Canadian man facing 14 murder charges to plead guilty to aiding suicide
events.summary: The lawyer for a Canadian man facing murder charges for allegedly selling lethal substances online to people at risk of self-harm has told the Canadian Broadcasting Corp. that he has agreed to plead guilty to counseling or aiding suicide

events.title: Gunman kills at least 5 in Kyiv, takes hostages; police shoot him dead
events.summary: Gunman kills at least 5 people in Kyiv street and takes hostages; police shoot him dead

events.title: Mexico arrests alleged drug trafficker considered one of Europe's most wanted
events.summary: He's wanted in Hungary on drug trafficking accusations, is sought by Interpol.

events.title: Helicopter crash on Indonesia's Borneo island kills 8
events.summary: A helicopter flying between palm oil plantations on Borneo Island has crashed, killing the eight people on board

events.title: Philippine troops kill 10 suspected Muslim militants in deadliest clash so far this year
events.summary: Philippine forces have killed 10 suspected Muslim militants in the deadliest clash so far this year with the remaining rebels waging a considerably weakened separatist insurgency

events.title: Costa Rica takes in a second group of migrants deported from the US
events.summary: Costa Rica has received a second group of migrants deported from the United States

events.title: Canada Post taking steps to stop home mail delivery
events.summary: The government-owned corporation that delivers mail in Canada is taking steps to stop home delivery

events.title: Nigeria students abducted as gunmen attack passenger bus in Benue state
events.summary: Gunmen have abducted students on their way to university examinations in Nigeria's Benue state

events.title: EU officials in Hungary to discuss unlocking billions of euros held while Orbán was in charge
events.summary: European Union officials are meeting Friday in Budapest with Hungary's incoming leader Péter Magyar's team

events.title: Brazil's Lula and Sánchez of Spain headline meetings of progressive leaders in Barcelona
events.summary: Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva is starting a two-day visit to Spain where he and his Spanish counterpart Pedro Sánchez will meet with other leaders, mostly of mid-to-small-sized countries, who are concerned with the fate of the democrati...

events.title: A monkey in Mexico, elections in Peru and other top photos from Latin America and the Caribbean
events.summary:
April 10–16, 2026

A baby monkey named Yuji, with a stuffed dog as a surrogate mom, stole hearts in Guadalajara, Mexico. Peruvians took to the polls to elect their ninth president in 10 years. In Colina, Chile, pilgrims on horseback took part in the Quasimodo Feast, a procession held the Sunday following Easter, accompanying priests giving communion to the poor.

events.summary: LONDON — British Prime Minister Keir Starmer probably wishes he had never heard the name Peter Mandelson

events.title: Oil plunges to lowest price in weeks after Iran says it will fully open Strait of Hormuz
events.summary: Oil prices plunged and two U.S. stock indexes hit all-time highs after Iran's foreign minister said the Strait of Hormuz was open for commercial vessels.

events.title: Pope Leo XIV greeted by crowd of 120,000 in Cameroon as he attacks uneven distribution of wealth
events.summary: Pope Leo XIV used a Mass attended by more than 100,000 people in Cameroon on Friday to openly criticize the uneven distribution of wealth.

events.title: Navy reservist arrested in manhunt after his wife's body was found in freezer
events.summary: A Navy reservist from Virginia who was accused of murdering his wife after her body was found in their freezer was arrested in Hong Kong, ending a two-month international manhunt, authorities said Wednesday

events.title: Netanyahu says Israel has not agreed to withdraw its forces from southern Lebanon
events.summary: President Donald Trump announced that the leaders of Israel and Lebanon had reached a ceasefire deal as fighting raged between Israel and the Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah.

events.title: Russian missiles and drones bombard Ukraine for hours, killing at least 18 people
events.summary: Russia unleashed a barrage of hundreds of drones and missiles against Ukraine overnight, killing at least 18 people in its most intense attack of the year.

events.title: Kenyan court jails Chinese man in ant-smuggling case
events.summary: A Kenyan court on Wednesday ordered a Chinese man to pay a fine of 1 million shillings ($7,746) and gave him a 12-month jail term for trying to smuggle live ants out of the country

events.title: U.S. military turned back 10 ships since start of Iranian port blockade
events.summary: During the first 24 hours of the U.S. blockade of Iranian ports, American warships issued warnings to six vessels, prompting them to turn around.

events.title: American YouTuber is jailed for 6 months in South Korea on public nuisance charge
events.summary: An American livestreamer who enraged South Korea by kissing a statue honoring women forced into wartime sexual slavery was jailed for six months on public nuisance and other charges.

events.title: Kanye West postpones France show after officials said he was 'not welcome'
events.summary: Ye has postponed a concert in France that was scheduled for this summer after officials in the country said he was not welcome and threatened to ban him over his antisemitic comments and songs

events.title: Tankers, talks and Trump's 'dangerous' blockade: China's Iran war involvement gets louder
events.summary: BEIJING — China has been a quiet — if crucial — player in the Iran war, as U.S. military action upends the global economy

events.title: U.S. and Iran could hold new peace talks as soon as this week, sources say
events.summary: A new round of in-person talks between the United States and Iran could be held as early as this week, two people familiar with the ongoing negotiations told NBC News
